items: Superintendent Search Discussion — Board discussed approach and timeline for national search following Dr. Baker's announced retirement. Reviewed community engagement plan, desired qualities, and process milestones.
ProTech Industrial Hygienist — $118,200 for industrial hygiene consulting during HVAC projects (asbestos/lead abatement oversight, air monitoring, clearance testing). Measure S Bond Fund.
One Life Counseling — $973,200 for mental health counseling and crisis intervention services across district schools for 2025-26
PowerFlex EV Charging — $184,400 for electric vehicle charging infrastructure at district sites
LCAP Readoption — technical readoption of the 2025-26 LCAP following budget revisions
6 Policy First Readings: media relations (BP 1112), social media (BP 1114), claims/actions against the district (BP 3320), immigration enforcement (BP/AR 5145.13), public statements (BB 9010)
Resolution No. 4 — Sole Source: PowerFlex as District standard EV charger software (Shell Recharge exited market April 2025; ~100 chargers, 7 electric buses need unified platform)
Resolution No. 5 — Authorize Technology Procurement (PCC 20118.2) for EV charging: $112K hardware replacement at 3 sites + $72.4K 3-year software = $184,360 total
Resolution No. 6 — Engage National Benefits Services (NBS) as TPA for 403(b)/457(b) retirement plans; projected to save 250+ staff hours/year
Resolution No. 7 — Ed Code and Title V Options for Teaching Assignments for 2025-26 (LAOs for middle school staffing)
ELOP Program Guide 2025-2026 — Expanded Learning Opportunities Program plan
College Advising Prep — math tutoring services
Kennedy Middle School field trip to Yosemite

llmResponse.agenda_changes: Item 8.2 (First Reading and Discussion of Board Policy 1112: Media Relations) was pulled from the agenda at the start of the meeting during 'Changes to the Agenda.' President Wells announced: 'I've got one request to still yeah to remove uh 8.2 which is the first reading in discussion of BP 1112 the media relations.' The board approved the agenda as amended.
llmResponse.consent_vote_words: The School Board approved the Consent Items (Márquez, Weekly; 5-0), taking the following action

items.intro_words: Um okay, so our first item is the discussion on the process for uh national search for the next superintendent of schools.
Approval of the Industrial Hygienist Services agreements with ProTech Consulting & Engineering for the HVAC Upgrade projects

Adoption of Resolution No. 6 to Engage a Third-Party Administrator for the District's existing 403b and 457b
First Reading and Discussion of Board Policy 1114: District-Sponsored Social Media
First Reading and Discussion of Board Policy 1114: District-Sponsored Social Media
First Reading and Discussion of Board Policy 3320: Claims And Actions Against The District
First Reading and Discussion of Board Policy and Administrative Regulation 5145.13: Response To Immigration Enforcement
First Reading and Discussion of Board Bylaw 9010: Public Statements
Approval of the School Assignments and Committee Assignments for Board Members for the 2025–26 School Year
Readoption of the Local Control and Accountability Plan (LCAP) for the 2025/2026 School Year
Approval of the Expanded Learning Opportunities Program Guide for 2025-2026
Adoption of Resolutions 4 and 5, Approval of Sole Sourcing Resolution and Resolution to Authorize Technology Procurement
Adoption of Resolution No. 7, Approval of Education Code and Title V Options for
